steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that most studies were conducted in North America and found one relationship between dissolved organic carbon (DOC) and mercury, but a study in Brazil found the opposite. The blank must explain why a different location produced a reversed finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Few of the studies conducted in North America have been able to measure dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels with the same level of precision as in Almeida, Vieira, and Nogueira's study.: suggests measurement tools lacked precision. The issue is not measurement error but a directional reversal in results. ✗
  • Choice B — Almeida, Vieira, and Nogueira's finding may differ from the findings of other studies due to a difference in environmental circumstances that affects the relationship between dissolved organic carbon and mercury in fresh water.: states that environmental conditions vary and affect the DOC–mercury relationship. This directly explains why Brazil's different environment produced the opposite result. ✓
  • Choice C — Almeida, Vieira, and Nogueira's study may have inadvertently measured a different characteristic of bodies of fresh water than their levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury.: addresses the scope of the studies. Scope differences do not explain a reversal in the direction of the relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — The mercury levels reported in Almeida, Vieira, and Nogueira's study were much higher than those reported in the study by Carter and colleagues even though the dissolved organic carbon levels reported in the two studies were approximately the same.: concerns absolute mercury levels rather than the direction of the association with DOC. ✗
That is the correct answer.
